summary: F370-F373 API helper extraction/docs are already complete.
Ack/finish services assert task-pull auth before DB mutation, but focused specs only cover enabled-token paths and lock mismatch behavior.
Next smallest API-only step is spec hardening for default-off/token-gated ack and finish lifecycle endpoints, with production API files read-only.
why_next: F370-F373 completed structural API task-pull splits, so another helper extraction is not the next smallest justified step. The remaining board/architect risk is contract drift plus default-off gate preservation; ack and finish already call auth before mutation, but their focused specs exercise enabled-token success/failure paths rather than proving rejected lifecycle requests cannot mutate jobs or sync linked runs. Adding only spec coverage is the narrowest API step and avoids all CLI overlap.
risks: Spec helper may need a small local override to construct disabled-token ConfigService without changing production files.
Exact thrown error text should not be over-specified unless already stable in existing auth tests.
API type-check may require prior build only if unrelated type artifacts are stale; executor should report that as verification environment noise, not expand scope.
